title: Password hashing with argon2 and pepper
description: Implement a shared password hashing utility using argon2id, combined with a server-side pepper from `ARGON2_SECRET`, used by both the bootstrap seed script and user creation/password reset flows.
acceptanceCriteria: `hashPassword(plain: string): Promise<string>` produces an argon2id hash incorporating `ARGON2_SECRET` as additional data (pepper)
`verifyPassword(plain: string, hash: string): Promise<boolean>` correctly validates matching and non-matching passwords
Hashing uses argon2id variant with reasonable memory/time cost defaults (e.g. `memoryCost: 19456, timeCost: 2` or library defaults tuned for a single-container deployment)
The pepper (`ARGON2_SECRET`) is read from env once at module load, never logged, never included in the stored hash string itself
Two calls to `hashPassword` with the same input produce different hashes (per-call random salt)
TASK-4's seed script and any future login/reset code use this utility instead of ad-hoc hashing

steps.details: Add `argon2` (node-argon2, which wraps a native argon2id implementation) as a dependency of `apps/server`.

Create `apps/server/src/auth/password.ts` exporting `hashPassword(plain: string)` that calls `argon2.hash(plain + ARGON2_SECRET, { type: argon2.argon2id })`. Read `ARGON2_SECRET` from `process.env` at module load and throw at startup if missing.
Add `verifyPassword(plain: string, hash: string)` calling `argon2.verify(hash, plain + ARGON2_SECRET)`, returning `false` on any thrown error rather than propagating it.
Update TASK-4's `apps/server/src/db/seed.ts` to call `hashPassword(INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD)` instead of any placeholder hashing.
Test: correct password verifies true; incorrect password verifies false; hashing the same password twice yields two different hash strings; hash format starts with `$argon2id$`.

technicalNotes: Never log `plain`, `hash`, or `ARGON2_SECRET` anywhere, including error messages.

Concatenating the pepper into the input string before argon2's own per-call salt is a simple, adequate pattern for a single-container deployment; a dedicated peppering scheme (e.g. HMAC pre-hash) is unnecessary added complexity for this MVP.
